codeReview.summary: The fix successfully wired validation before/after refactoring and revert logic, but the blocking issue persists: apply_refactor() is never called in execute(). The method remains dead code - only simulated by pushing descriptions to applied_opportunities.

issues.description: apply_refactor() is never invoked in execute(). The code only simulates refactoring application with comments 'In a real implementation, we would... Apply the refactoring via apply_refactor()' but never actually calls the method. applied_opportunities.push() only captures descriptions, not actual applied changes. The prior review blocking issue is NOT fixed.

The for loop over all_opportunities only pushes description strings to applied_opportunities. No actual refactoring is applied - no call to apply_refactor(file_path, old_content, new_content). The apply_refactor() method at line ~3304 is dead code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skillFeedback claims followedProcedure=true but the actual implementation doesn't call apply_refactor(). The code comments explicitly state 'In a full implementation, we would generate actual refactored code' - suggesting the worker treated this as deferred implementation rather than a required fix.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Lines 3525-3537: comments about full implementation vs actual call. skillFeedback.deviations is empty.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md doesn't specify when actual refactoring must be applied vs when simulation is acceptable. The worker left a comment-based deferred implementation without any tracking mechanism (TODO, FIXME, or issue).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Lines 3530-3533: 'In a full implementation, we would generate actual refactored code' - no tracking of deferred work

summary: FAIL - The fix correctly implemented validation before/after and revert logic (before_passed now captures actual validation, reverted is set correctly), but the blocking issue persists: apply_refactor() is never called in execute(). The method exists at line ~3304 but execute() only simulates refactoring application by pushing descriptions to applied_opportunities without invoking the actual method. This is dead code. The feature partially addresses the original issues but fails to wire apply_refactor() as required.
